name: NSP Post-Matric Scholarship for SC Students
provider: Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Govt. of India
eligibility: SC students studying post-matric; family income below ₹2.5 lakh/year

name: NSP Post-Matric Scholarship for ST Students
provider: Ministry of Tribal Affairs, Govt. of India
eligibility: ST students studying post-matric; family income below ₹2.5 lakh/year

name: NSP Scholarship for Minority Communities
provider: Ministry of Minority Affairs, Govt. of India
eligibility: Muslim, Christian, Sikh, Buddhist, Jain, or Zoroastrian students; income below ₹2 lakh/year; min 50% marks

name: Central Sector Scholarship (CSS) Scheme
provider: Ministry of Education, Govt. of India
eligibility: Top 20 percentile in Class 12 board exams; family income below ₹8 lakh/year; enrolled in regular degree course

name: Pragati Scholarship for Girl Students (Technical)
eligibility: Girl students admitted to AICTE-approved diploma/degree programs; family income below ₹8 lakh/year

name: Saksham Scholarship for Differently-Abled Students
eligibility: Differently-abled students in AICTE-approved diploma/degree programs; family income below ₹8 lakh/year

name: HDFC Educational Crisis Scholarship
eligibility: Students from Classes 6–12 or pursuing graduation/PG; family facing financial crisis (COVID-19, death of breadwinner, etc.); income below ₹5 lakh/year

name: Sitaram Jindal Foundation Scholarship
eligibility: Students from Class 10 onward to PG; family income below ₹3 lakh/year; min 55% marks

name: Tata Capital Pankh Scholarship Programme
eligibility: Students pursuing professional/technical courses (Class 11–Degree); income below ₹4 lakh/year; min 60% marks

name: Vidyasaarathi — L&T ECC Scholarship
eligibility: Civil/Mechanical/Electrical engineering students in 2nd–4th year; min 60% marks; income below ₹6 lakh/year

name: Inspire Scholarship (MHRD)
provider: Department of Science & Technology, Govt. of India
eligibility: Top 1% scorers in Class 12 pursuing B.Sc./B.S./Int. M.Sc. in Natural Sciences; age below 22

name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMS)
provider: Ministry of Education, Govt. of India
eligibility: Class 9–12 students from government schools; family income below ₹3.5 lakh/year; pass NMMS exam

name: Begum Hazrat Mahal National Scholarship
eligibility: Girl students from minority communities in Class 9–12; min 50% marks; income below ₹2 lakh/year

name: PM Scholarship Scheme for RPF/RPSF
provider: Ministry of Railways, Govt. of India
eligibility: Wards/widows of RPF/RPSF personnel; pursuing 1st year of regular professional/technical degree; min 60% in Class 12

name: Swami Vivekananda Merit-cum-Means Scholarship (West Bengal)
eligibility: West Bengal students in UG/PG programs; family income below ₹2.5 lakh/year; min 75% in Class 12

name: Mukhyamantri Medhavi Vidyarthi Yojana (Madhya Pradesh)
eligibility: MP domicile; min 70% in Class 12 (CBSE/ICSE) or 85% (MP Board); admitted to govt./selected private colleges

name: College Board 90% Fee Waiver Program
eligibility: Class 11 and 12 students preparing for SAT/AP exams

name: vivo KanyaGyaan Scholarship Program 2025-26
eligibility: Girl students in UG/PG programs; min 60% marks; income below ₹6 lakh/year

name: vivo Punjab-Vidya Setu Scholarship 2026
eligibility: Students domiciled in Punjab, studying in any stream

name: IET India Scholarship Award 2026
eligibility: Engineering students in penultimate year; academic excellence + extracurricular achievements

name: Reliance Foundation Undergraduate Scholarship
eligibility: Students in 1st year of UG programs (STEM or Humanities); min 60% in Class 12; income below ₹15 lakh/year

name: Kishore Vaigyanik Protsahan Yojana (KVPY)
provider: Indian Institute of Science (IISc), Dept. of Science & Technology
eligibility: Students in Class 11–1st year of B.Sc./B.S./Int. M.Sc.; pass KVPY aptitude test + interview

name: nurtr Nurturing Minds with Chess Program (90% Fee Waiver)
eligibility: School/college students aged 5 to 25 years

name: Dr. Ambedkar Post-Matric Scholarship for OBC Students
provider: Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment, Govt. of India
eligibility: OBC students pursuing post-matric education; family income below ₹1 lakh/year (central list OBC)

name: Buddy4Study's Online Degree Scholarship Support Programme
eligibility: Students interested in pursuing accredited online degree programs
